programme.title: PL MOMENTS EP07: KOLO TOURE
programme.sub-title: Former Premier League enforcer Kolo Toure joins Will Perry in the locker room as he unveils his top 10 Premier League moments.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP39: AARON RAMSEY
programme.sub-title: From starting out playing rugby to winning multiple FA Cups with Arsenal, Aaron Ramsey talks to The Big Interview about the importance of grassroots football, the grounding that helped him pave the way to becoming a professional. Now in charge of the club closest to his heart, Cardiff City, Ramsey reflects on the overwhelming experience of joining Arsenal at just 17 years of age, the impact of the shocking leg-break which nearly ended his career in 2010 and the challenges he has faced since returning to the Welsh capital.
programme.desc: From starting out playing rugby to winning multiple FA Cups with Arsenal, Aaron Ramsey talks to The Big Interview about the importance of grassroots football, the grounding that helped him pave the way to becoming a professional. Now in charge of the club closest to his heart, Cardiff City, Ramsey reflects on the overwhelming experience of joining Arsenal at just 17 years of age, the impact of the shocking leg-break which nearly ended his career in 2010 and the challenges he has faced since returning to the Welsh capital.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP49: INSIDE MATTERS
programme.sub-title: In this powerful and deeply personal edition of PL Stories, former Premier League footballer and mental health advocate Clarke Carlisle sits down with players past and present to explore the hidden struggles behind life at the top of the game. From the pressures of expectation to the pain of isolation, Jason McAteer, Phil Jones, Eberechi Eze and Steven Caulker open up about their journeys with mental health—sharing raw, honest accounts of their lowest moments and the strength it took to rise again.
programme.desc: In this powerful and deeply personal edition of PL Stories, former Premier League footballer and mental health advocate Clarke Carlisle sits down with players past and present to explore the hidden struggles behind life at the top of the game. From the pressures of expectation to the pain of isolation, Jason McAteer, Phil Jones, Eberechi Eze and Steven Caulker open up about their journeys with mental health—sharing raw, honest accounts of their lowest moments and the strength it took to rise again.

programme.sub-title: A true Premier League icon, Alan Shearer recalls his career at his hometown club Newcastle United. programme.sub-title: A true Premier League icon, Alan Shearer recalls his career at his hometown club Newcastle United. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP39: AARON RAMSEY
programme.sub-title: From starting out playing rugby to winning multiple FA Cups with Arsenal, Aaron Ramsey talks to The Big Interview about the importance of grassroots football, the grounding that helped him pave the way to becoming a professional. Now in charge of the club closest to his heart, Cardiff City, Ramsey reflects on the overwhelming experience of joining Arsenal at just 17 years of age, the impact of the shocking leg-break which nearly ended his career in 2010 and the challenges he has faced since returning to the Welsh capital.
programme.desc: From starting out playing rugby to winning multiple FA Cups with Arsenal, Aaron Ramsey talks to The Big Interview about the importance of grassroots football, the grounding that helped him pave the way to becoming a professional. Now in charge of the club closest to his heart, Cardiff City, Ramsey reflects on the overwhelming experience of joining Arsenal at just 17 years of age, the impact of the shocking leg-break which nearly ended his career in 2010 and the challenges he has faced since returning to the Welsh capital.

programme.title: MATCHDAY LIVE Newcastle United v Chelsea / Nottingham Forest v Leicester City / Liverpool v Arsenal
programme.sub-title: A bumper Premier League Sunday begins with a huge European clash as Champions League chasers Newcastle and Chelsea face off at St James’ Park. That’s followed by the East Midlands derby between Nottingham Forest and Leicester. The day is rounded off with first against second as Liverpool take on Arsenal at Anfield.
programme.desc: A bumper Premier League Sunday begins with a huge European clash as Champions League chasers Newcastle and Chelsea face off at St James’ Park. That’s followed by the East Midlands derby between Nottingham Forest and Leicester. The day is rounded off with first against second as Liverpool take on Arsenal at Anfield.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP49: INSIDE MATTERS
programme.sub-title: In this powerful and deeply personal edition of PL Stories, former Premier League footballer and mental health advocate Clarke Carlisle sits down with players past and present to explore the hidden struggles behind life at the top of the game. From the pressures of expectation to the pain of isolation, Jason McAteer, Phil Jones, Eberechi Eze and Steven Caulker open up about their journeys with mental health—sharing raw, honest accounts of their lowest moments and the strength it took to rise again.
programme.desc: In this powerful and deeply personal edition of PL Stories, former Premier League footballer and mental health advocate Clarke Carlisle sits down with players past and present to explore the hidden struggles behind life at the top of the game. From the pressures of expectation to the pain of isolation, Jason McAteer, Phil Jones, Eberechi Eze and Steven Caulker open up about their journeys with mental health—sharing raw, honest accounts of their lowest moments and the strength it took to rise again.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP50: CHRIS WOOD
programme.sub-title: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he’s become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP38: (Subbed) AARON RAMSEY
programme.sub-title: After a meteoric rise through Cardiff’s youth system, Aaron Ramsey’s dream of playing for one of the world’s biggest clubs was realised. programme.sub-title: We look back on the extraordinary career of Mohamed Salah, tracing his rise from a small village in Egypt to global football stardom. programme.title: PL STORIES EP26: THE GREATEST GAME EVER PLAYED
programme.sub-title: We gather a panel of experts to tackle a nearly impossible question: What truly is the Greatest Premier League Game Ever? Join two ex-footballers, a seasoned commentator, and a top football journalist as they lock horns in a passionate roundtable discussion. The panel revisits iconic moments, dissecting tactics, and legendary performances. Expect banter, bold claims, and maybe even a few surprises, as they strive to settle the debate—or perhaps keep it alive. Whether reliving “Fergie Time” magic, Leicester’s miracle season, or Aguero’s legendary finish, this episode celebrates the unfiltered drama that only Premier League history can offer.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP20: MCI v QPR 11/12
programme.sub-title: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.
programme.desc: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP06: TIM SHERWOOD: MY GREATEST SEASON
programme.sub-title: Blackburn Rovers' Premier League title win in 1994/95 was a historic achievement. Under the guidance of Kenny Dalglish, and fuelled by the prolific partnership of Alan Shearer and Chris Sutton, the small-town club dethroned Manchester United and reached the pinnacle of English football. Captain Tim Sherwood recalls the key moments.
programme.desc: Blackburn Rovers' Premier League title win in 1994/95 was a historic achievement. Under the guidance of Kenny Dalglish, and fuelled by the prolific partnership of Alan Shearer and Chris Sutton, the small-town club dethroned Manchester United and reached the pinnacle of English football. Captain Tim Sherwood recalls the key moments.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP38: (Subbed) AARON RAMSEY
programme.sub-title: After a meteoric rise through Cardiff’s youth system, Aaron Ramsey’s dream of playing for one of the world’s biggest clubs was realised. But just as his career was reaching new heights, a devastating double leg break threatened to bring it all crashing down. The Wales captain takes us through his footballing journey to date.
programme.desc: After a meteoric rise through Cardiff’s youth system, Aaron Ramsey’s dream of playing for one of the world’s biggest clubs was realised. But just as his career was reaching new heights, a devastating double leg break threatened to bring it all crashing down. The Wales captain takes us through his footballing journey to date.

programme.title: PL MOMENTS EP14: ROBERT PIRES
programme.sub-title: PL Moments combines archive and first person testimony to revisit the very best games, goals, players and performances from the last 30 years.
programme.desc: PL Moments combines archive and first person testimony to revisit the very best games, goals, players and performances from the last 30 years.

programme.title: MATCH OFFICIALS MIC'D UP TUES 13 MAY
programme.sub-title: Match Officials Mic’d Up returns for a ninth episode of the 2024/25 season, with PGMOL Chief Refereeing Officer Howard Webb joining Michael Owen to discuss key decisions from recent Premier League Matchweeks, using previously un-released audio between the on-field officials and VAR teams.
programme.desc: Match Officials Mic’d Up returns for a ninth episode of the 2024/25 season, with PGMOL Chief Refereeing Officer Howard Webb joining Michael Owen to discuss key decisions from recent Premier League Matchweeks, using previously un-released audio between the on-field officials and VAR teams.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP50: CHRIS WOOD
programme.sub-title: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he’s become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.
programme.desc: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. programme.title: PL REWIND EP12: JERMAIN DEFOE: MY TOTTENHAM YEARS
programme.sub-title: Jermain Defoe scored 162 Premier League goals, and it was at Tottenham where he was at his prolific best. The striker recalls his two spells with the North London club, including the memorable occasion where he hit 5 in one match.
